WYLIE.source: GNIS point on the NC side (Mecklenburg/Gaston Co.); the dam itself is in York Co., SC
WYLIE.notes: Lake Wylie spans the NC/SC line - this point is on the NC portion, not the dam.

FISHINGCRK.source: Wikipedia - wholly in Lancaster/Chester Co., SC

DEARBORN.source: Duke Energy illumination article - wholly in Chester Co., SC (this is Great Falls Reservoir)

CEDARCREEK.source: Duke Energy Catawba-Wateree recreation materials - wholly in Chester Co., SC

WATEREE.source: Wikipedia - wholly in Kershaw/Fairfield/Lancaster Co., SC

99ISLAND.source: Wikipedia (Broad River) / SC GNIS - wholly in Cherokee Co., SC, does not back up into NC

BADCREEK.source: Wikipedia - wholly in Oconee Co., SC

JOCASSEE.source: Wikipedia - wholly in Oconee/Pickens Co., SC

KEOWEE.source: Wikipedia - wholly in Oconee Co., SC

ROBINSON.source: Duke Energy's own site (duke-energy.com/community/lakes/services/lake-robinson-guidelines) confirms this is the H.B. Robinson Nuclear Plant cooling lake near Hartsville, Darlington/Chesterfield Co., SC - not a NC lake despite the generic name.

TUCKASEGEE.source: Confirmed to exist and be in NC (small ~7.9-acre reservoir on West Fork Tuckasegee River, Jackson Co., per Duke's FERC P-2686 licensing docs) but no confidently-sourced precise coordinate found. Needs a NID or FERC exhibit-map lookup before including.
